case_title: IN RE: STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

PETITION FILED BY PETITIONER STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION AS TO RESPONDENT GOLDEN GATE BANK

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S PETITION FOR HEARING ON THIRD PARTY CLAIM. THIRD PARTY CLAIM IS NOT VALID GIVEN CLAIMANT'S FAILURE TO CARRY BURDEN OF PROVING HIS CONTROL AS A SECURED PARTY OF THE ACCOUNT WITHIN COMMERCIAL CODE 9104. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

calendar.matters:
Petition Of Hearing And Petition For Hearing On Third Party Claim And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Oct-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristian D. Whitten of the Attorney General's Office, 703-5589, appearing for petitioner and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court made a personal disclosure, and no objections were raised. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S Petition For Hearing On Third Party Claim. Third party claim is not valid given claimant's failure to carry burden of proving his control as a secured party of the account within Commercial Code 9104. Order signed in open Court. Judgment signed after hearing. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
